page.title: Marbella Ironman 2028: Yacht Recovery Charters for Athletes

data.meta_description: Book a yacht recovery charter for Marbella Ironman 2028. Relax on the water post-race with our fleet starting at €749/2h. Limited availability – enquire today.
data.summary: Marbella Ironman 2028 athletes can recover in style aboard our private yacht charters from Puerto Banús. Post-race relaxation with licensed skipper, drinks, and snacks included – no crowds, just the Mediterranean.
key_facts: Marbella Ironman 2028 swim leg likely starts from the Golden Mile; finish line near Puerto Banús.
Yacht recovery charters depart from Puerto Banús – minutes from the finish area.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h, all-inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h, include a free jet ski.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Maximum pax per boat ranges from 5 (Dubhe) to 12 (Ferretti 94).
Book early – Ironman weekend sees high demand for all Marbella marinas.

faq.q: What is a yacht recovery charter for Ironman athletes?
faq.a: A private boat trip immediately after your race, designed for relaxation, muscle recovery, and celebration. You skip the crowded finish area and head straight onto the water with cold drinks, snacks, and a licensed skipper.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a small group of athletes?
faq.a: For 5–8 people, the Dubhe (5 pax) or Bandido (8 pax) are ideal. For up to 10–12, the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, or Fairline Targa 12m work well. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book for Ironman 2028?
faq.a: As early as possible – ideally 6–12 months ahead. Ironman weekend fills every berth in Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and Cabopino. Early booking guarantees availability and your preferred boat.

faq.q: Is the skipper included in the price?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

faq.q: Can we swim or use a jet ski during the charter?
faq.a: Absolutely. All Mangusta 80 charters (Tier B) include a free jet ski. Other boats can stop for swimming at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés – just ask the skipper.

faq.q: What if I’m not an athlete but my partner is?
faq.a: Perfect for support crews. You can watch the finish from the boat, then pick up your athlete directly from the dock. The boat becomes your private recovery lounge.

faq.q: Do you offer longer charters for full-day recovery?
faq.a: Yes. Tier A boats go up to 8 hours (€2,299). For larger groups, Tier B Mangusta 80 starts at 4 hours. All other boats are priced on request – contact us via WhatsApp for custom durations.

faq.q: Where does the charter depart from?
faq.a: All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, the luxury marina just east of Marbella town. It’s a 5-minute walk from the Ironman finish line and has ample parking and taxi ranks.

data.body_html:
The Marbella Ironman 2028 is set to push thousands of athletes to their limits – and afterwards, nothing beats a private yacht charter for recovery. Instead of queuing for a post-race drink or fighting for a sun lounger, you can step straight onto your own boat at Puerto Banús, just metres from the finish line. With cold drinks, soft seats, and a licensed skipper handling everything, you focus on relaxing your muscles while the Mediterranean does the rest.

Why Choose a Yacht Recovery Charter for Marbella Ironman 2028?

Post-race recovery isn’t just about foam rollers and protein shakes – it’s about mental reset. A private charter lets you float away from the crowds, with the sound of water replacing the finish-line PA. Our boats come fully crewed and stocked, so you don’t lift a finger. Whether you’ve just swum 3.8 km, cycled 180 km, or run a marathon, the gentle motion of the sea aids circulation and reduces stiffness.

Our Fleet for Athletes and Support Crew

We operate a range of boats from Puerto Banús, all within walking distance of the Ironman finish. For smaller groups, the Dubhe (8 m, 5 pax) or Bandido (11.5 m, 8 pax) are nimble and cost-effective. Mid-size options like the Astondoa 40 (12.5 m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5 m, 11 pax) offer more space for support crews. For the full experience, the Mangusta 80 (24 m, 12 pax) comes with a free jet ski – great for athletes who want a gentle water-based cool-down or a celebratory spin.

Pricing That Fits Your Race Budget

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). No surprises.

BoatDurationPrice
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 392 hours€749
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 398 hours€2,299
Mangusta 80 (any variant)4 hours minimum€4,719 (jet ski included)
All other boatsCustomPricing on request via WhatsApp

For larger groups, the Ferretti 94 (29 m, 12 pax) and Canados 86 (26 m, 12 pax) offer premium space. Contact us for a quote.

Best Recovery Spots Along the Costa del Sol

Your skipper knows the calmest anchorages for post-race relaxation. Within 15 minutes of Puerto Banús, you can drop anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés – sheltered coves with clear water. For a longer cruise, head west to Estepona or Sotogrande, where the sea is usually flat and the views of La Concha mountain are stunning. If you’d rather stay close, the coastline between Marbella Marina and Cabopino offers plenty of quiet spots to float and rehydrate.

What’s Included in Every Charter

  • Licensed skipper (English & Spanish speaking)
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) – no hidden taxes

How to Book Your Ironman Recovery Charter

Demand for Ironman weekend is high – many boats are reserved months in advance. To secure your preferred vessel, message us on WhatsApp with your group size, preferred date, and duration. We’ll confirm availability and send a payment link. For Tier A boats, you can book directly online. For all others, we’ll provide a custom quote within hours.

Don’t wait until race week – browse our full fleet and start planning your post-Ironman recovery today.

Frequently asked questions

What is a yacht recovery charter for Ironman athletes?

A private boat trip immediately after your race, designed for relaxation, muscle recovery, and celebration. You skip the crowded finish area and head straight onto the water with cold drinks, snacks, and a licensed skipper.

Which boats are best for a small group of athletes?

For 5–8 people, the Dubhe (5 pax) or Bandido (8 pax) are ideal. For up to 10–12, the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, or Fairline Targa 12m work well. All depart from Puerto Banús.

How far in advance should I book for Ironman 2028?

As early as possible – ideally 6–12 months ahead. Ironman weekend fills every berth in Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and Cabopino. Early booking guarantees availability and your preferred boat.

Is the skipper included in the price?

Yes, every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

Can we swim or use a jet ski during the charter?

Absolutely. All Mangusta 80 charters (Tier B) include a free jet ski. Other boats can stop for swimming at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés – just ask the skipper.

What if I’m not an athlete but my partner is?

Perfect for support crews. You can watch the finish from the boat, then pick up your athlete directly from the dock. The boat becomes your private recovery lounge.

Do you offer longer charters for full-day recovery?

Yes. Tier A boats go up to 8 hours (€2,299). For larger groups, Tier B Mangusta 80 starts at 4 hours. All other boats are priced on request – contact us via WhatsApp for custom durations.

Where does the charter depart from?

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, the luxury marina just east of Marbella town. It’s a 5-minute walk from the Ironman finish line and has ample parking and taxi ranks.

Book your Marbella Ironman 2028 yacht recovery charter now – limited availability.
